sentences.sentence: Microbes, most of them bacteria, have populated this planet since long before animal life developed and they will outlive us.
sentences.translation: 微生物——其中大多数是细菌——早在动物生命出现之前就已经遍布这个星球，而且它们会比人类存在得更久。

structure.text: since long before animal life developed

difficult_points: most of them bacteria 是插入的同位语，补充说明微生物中大多数是细菌。
since 引导时间状语，long before 强调微生物出现得远早于动物生命。
outlive us 表示“比我们活得更久”，此处泛指比人类存在得更久。

sentences.sentence: Invisible to the naked eye, they are ubiquitous.
sentences.translation: 它们肉眼不可见，却无处不在。
difficult_points: 句首形容词短语说明主语 they 的状态，相当于 although they are invisible to the naked eye。
ubiquitous 表示“无处不在的”，与前面的“肉眼不可见”形成对照。

sentences.sentence: They inhabit the soil, air, rocks and water and are present within every form of life, from seaweed and coral to dogs and humans.
sentences.translation: 它们栖居于土壤、空气、岩石和水中，也存在于各种生命体内，从海藻和珊瑚到狗和人类，无一例外。

structure.text: inhabit the soil, air, rocks and water
and are present within every form of life
from seaweed and coral to dogs and humans

difficult_points: inhabit 和 are present 是由 and 连接、共同以 They 为主语的两个并列谓语。
from ... to ... 列举 every form of life 的范围，而不是表示实际移动。

sentences.sentence: And, as Yong explains in his utterly absorbing and hugely important book, we mess with them at our peril.
sentences.translation: 而且，正如 Yong 在他那本极其引人入胜且意义重大的书中所解释的那样，我们干扰微生物是要自担风险的。

structure.text: as Yong explains in his utterly absorbing and hugely important book

difficult_points: as 引导方式状语从句，意为“正如 Yong 所解释的那样”。
mess with 并非单纯“弄乱”，这里指不恰当地干扰或摆弄。
at our peril 表示这样做会让我们自己承担危险后果。

sentences.sentence: Every species has its own colony of microbes, called a 'microbiome', and these microbes vary not only between species but also between individuals and within different parts of each individual.
sentences.translation: 每个物种都有自己的一群微生物，称为“微生物组”；这些微生物不仅因物种而异，也因个体而异，甚至在同一个体的不同部位也不相同。

structure.text: Every species has its own colony of microbes
not only between species but also between individuals and within different parts of each individual

difficult_points: called a 'microbiome' 是过去分词短语，修饰 colony of microbes。
not only ... but also ... 连接微生物发生差异的多个范围。
within different parts of each individual 表明同一个体内部的微生物也会因部位不同而变化。

expressions.meaning_zh: 一群微生物；微生物群落

sentences.sentence: What is amazing is that while the number of human cells in the average person is about 30 trillion, the number of microbial ones is higher – about 39 trillion.
sentences.translation: 令人惊讶的是，普通人体内约有30万亿个人体细胞，而微生物细胞的数量更多，约为39万亿个。

structure.text: that while the number of human cells in the average person is about 30 trillion, the number of microbial ones is higher – about 39 trillion
while the number of human cells in the average person is about 30 trillion

difficult_points: What is amazing 是 what 引导的主语从句，整体表示“令人惊讶的事情”。
that 引导表语从句，说明具体令人惊讶的内容。
while 在这里表示对比而非时间；ones 代替前文的 cells。

sentences.sentence: At best, Yong informs us, we are only 50 per cent human.
sentences.translation: Yong 告诉我们，即便按最乐观的情况算，我们也只有50%是人类细胞。

structure.text: we are only 50 per cent human

difficult_points: Yong informs us 是插入语，主干是 we are only 50 per cent human。
at best 表示“充其量、即使按最好的情况”，带有限定语气。
50 per cent human 是概括性说法，指人体细胞与微生物数量的比例。

expressions.meaning_zh: 充其量；在最好的情况下

sentences.sentence: Indeed, some scientists even suggest we should think of each species and its microbes as a single unit, dubbed a 'holobiont'.
sentences.translation: 事实上，一些科学家甚至提出，我们应该把每个物种及其微生物视为一个整体，并将其称为“共生整体”。

structure.text: we should think of each species and its microbes as a single unit

difficult_points: suggest 后省略了 that，其后的完整句子作宾语从句。
think of A as B 表示“把 A 看作 B”。
dubbed a 'holobiont' 是过去分词短语，补充说明这个 single unit 的名称。

sentences.sentence: In each human there are microbes that live only in the stomach, the mouth or the armpit and by and large they do so peacefully.
sentences.translation: 每个人体内都有一些只生活在胃、口腔或腋窝中的微生物，而且总体而言，它们都能安稳共存。

structure.text: that live only in the stomach, the mouth or the armpit
and by and large they do so peacefully

difficult_points: that 引导定语从句，修饰 microbes。
do so 代替前文的 live，避免重复。
by and large 是插入式状语，表示“总的来说”。

sentences.sentence: So 'bad' microbes are just microbes out of context.
sentences.translation: 所以，所谓的“坏”微生物，其实只是出现在了不合适环境中的微生物。
difficult_points: bad 加引号表示这里的“坏”并非微生物固有的绝对属性。
out of context 原指“脱离语境”，此处引申为处在不合适的生物环境中。

expressions.meaning_zh: 脱离适当环境；脱离语境

sentences.sentence: Microbes that sit contentedly in the human gut (where there are more microbes than there are stars in the galaxy) can become deadly if they find their way into the bloodstream.
sentences.translation: 那些安然存在于人体肠道中的微生物——肠道里的微生物甚至比银河系中的恒星还多——一旦进入血液，就可能变得致命。

structure.text: that sit contentedly in the human gut
where there are more microbes than there are stars in the galaxy
if they find their way into the bloodstream

difficult_points: that 引导定语从句修饰 Microbes，括号中的 where 从句又进一步说明 human gut。
more ... than ... 比较的是肠道微生物数量和银河系恒星数量。
find their way into 并非主动“寻找道路”，而是指最终进入某处。

sentences.sentence: These communities are constantly changing too.
sentences.translation: 这些微生物群落也在不断变化。
difficult_points: These communities 回指上文提到的人体内微生物群落。
现在进行时配合 constantly 强调变化是持续发生的。

sentences.sentence: The right hand shares just one sixth of its microbes with the left hand.
sentences.translation: 右手与左手共有的微生物仅占右手微生物的六分之一。

structure.text: just one sixth of its microbes

difficult_points: share A with B 表示“与 B 共有 A”。
its 指代 the right hand，one sixth of its microbes 是共有微生物所占的比例。

sentences.sentence: And, of course, we are surrounded by microbes.
sentences.translation: 当然，我们周围也充满了微生物。
difficult_points: of course 是插入语，用来承接并强调这一显而易见的事实。
are surrounded by 是被动结构，表示微生物存在于我们四周。

sentences.sentence: Every time we eat, we swallow a million microbes in each gram of food; we are continually swapping microbes with other humans, pets and the world at large.
sentences.translation: 我们每次进食，每克食物都会让我们吞下约一百万个微生物；与此同时，我们也一直在与其他人、宠物以及整个外界交换微生物。

structure.text: we swallow a million microbes in each gram of food
we are continually swapping microbes with other humans, pets and the world at large

difficult_points: Every time 引导时间状语从句，意为“每当……”。
分号连接两个关系紧密但语法上独立的分句。
the world at large 指周围更广泛的外部世界，而非某个具体地点。

sentences.sentence: It's a fascinating topic and Yong, a young British science journalist, is an extraordinarily adept guide.
sentences.translation: 这是一个引人入胜的话题，而年轻的英国科学记者 Yong 是一位极其高明的向导。

structure.text: and Yong, a young British science journalist, is an extraordinarily adept guide

difficult_points: and 连接两个完整的并列分句，分别评价主题和作者。
a young British science journalist 是 Yong 的同位语。
guide 在这里是比喻，指带领读者理解复杂主题的人。

sentences.sentence: Writing with lightness and panache, he has a knack of explaining complex science in terms that are both easy to understand and totally enthralling.
sentences.translation: 他的文笔轻松而有感染力，擅长用既浅显易懂又十分吸引人的方式解释复杂的科学知识。

structure.text: has a knack of explaining complex science
that are both easy to understand and totally enthralling

difficult_points: Writing ... 是现在分词短语，其逻辑主语是 he，说明写作方式。
have a knack of 表示具有做某事的天赋或诀窍。
that 引导定语从句修饰 terms，both ... and ... 连接两个并列表语。

expressions.meaning_zh: 以轻松而有感染力的方式

sentences.sentence: Yong is on a mission.
sentences.translation: Yong 正肩负着一项使命。
difficult_points: be on a mission 是固定表达，指带着明确使命行动。
具体使命由下一句说明：让读者像他一样喜爱微生物。

sentences.sentence: Leading us gently by the hand, he takes us into the world of microbes – a bizarre, alien planet – in a bid to persuade us to love them as much as he does.
sentences.translation: 他仿佛轻轻牵着我们的手，带我们走进微生物的世界——一个奇异而陌生的星球——试图说服我们像他一样热爱这些微生物。

structure.text: Leading us gently by the hand
he takes us into the world of microbes
in a bid to persuade us
to love them as much as he does

difficult_points: Leading ... 是现在分词短语，以“牵手带路”比喻作者对读者的引导。
破折号间的 a bizarre, alien planet 是 world of microbes 的同位语。
does 代替 loves them，as much as 表示程度相同。

expressions.meaning_zh: 和……一样多；达到同等程度

sentences.sentence: By the end, we do.
sentences.translation: 读到最后，我们确实也爱上了它们。
difficult_points: do 代替上一句的 love them，避免重复。
By the end 指读完整本书时，而不是泛指某段时间结束。

sentences.sentence: For most of human history we had no idea that microbes existed.
sentences.translation: 在人类历史的大部分时期，我们根本不知道微生物的存在。
difficult_points: For most of human history 是时间状语，表示跨度而非目的。
that microbes existed 是同位语性质的从句，说明 idea 的具体内容。

expressions.meaning_zh: 在人类历史的大部分时期

sentences.sentence: The first man to see these extraordinarily potent creatures was a Dutch lens-maker called Antony van Leeuwenhoek in the 1670s.
sentences.translation: 17世纪70年代，第一位看到这些力量非凡的生物的人，是一位名叫 Antony van Leeuwenhoek 的荷兰透镜制造者。

structure.text: to see these extraordinarily potent creatures

difficult_points: to see ... 是不定式作后置定语，修饰 The first man。
called ... 是过去分词短语，修饰 a Dutch lens-maker。
the 1670s 表示17世纪70年代，而不是1670年这一个年份。

sentences.sentence: Using microscopes of his own design that could magnify up to 270 times, he examined a drop of water from a nearby lake and found it teeming with tiny creatures he called 'animalcules'.
sentences.translation: 他使用自己设计、最高可放大270倍的显微镜，观察了附近湖泊的一滴水，发现其中挤满了他称为“微小动物”的微型生物。

structure.text: Using microscopes of his own design
that could magnify up to 270 times
he examined a drop of water from a nearby lake
and found it teeming with tiny creatures

difficult_points: Using ... 是方式状语，其逻辑主语为 he；that 从句修饰 microscopes。
found it teeming 是“find + 宾语 + 宾补”结构，teeming 描述水滴中的状态。
he called 'animalcules' 是省略关系代词的定语从句，修饰 tiny creatures。

sentences.sentence: It wasn't until nearly two hundred years later that the research of French biologist Louis Pasteur indicated that some microbes caused disease.
sentences.translation: 直到将近两百年后，法国生物学家 Louis Pasteur 的研究才表明，某些微生物会导致疾病。

structure.text: It wasn't until nearly two hundred years later that
the research of French biologist Louis Pasteur

difficult_points: It wasn't until ... that ... 是强调结构，意为“直到……才……”。
French biologist Louis Pasteur 是 Louis Pasteur 的身份说明。
第二个 that 引导 indicated 的宾语从句。

sentences.sentence: It was Pasteur's 'germ theory' that gave bacteria the poor image that endures today.
sentences.translation: 正是 Pasteur 的“细菌致病理论”使细菌背上了延续至今的负面形象。
difficult_points: It was ... that ... 是强调句，突出造成负面形象的是 Pasteur 的理论。
give bacteria the poor image 是双宾语结构，可理解为“赋予细菌负面形象”。
第二个 that 引导定语从句，修饰 the poor image。

sentences.sentence: Yong's book is in many ways a plea for microbial tolerance, pointing out that while fewer than one hundred species of bacteria bring disease, many thousands more play a vital role in maintaining our health.
sentences.translation: Yong 的书在许多方面都在呼吁人们宽容对待微生物；书中指出，致病的细菌种类不到一百种，而另有成千上万种细菌对维持我们的健康起着至关重要的作用。

structure.text: Yong's book is in many ways a plea for microbial tolerance
that while fewer than one hundred species of bacteria bring disease, many thousands more play a vital role in maintaining our health
while fewer than one hundred species of bacteria bring disease

difficult_points: pointing out ... 是现在分词短语，补充说明这本书提出的核心观点。
while 在宾语从句中表示对比：少数致病，更多种类有益。
many thousands more 中 more 后省略了 species of bacteria。

expressions.meaning_zh: 在……中发挥至关重要的作用

sentences.sentence: The book also acknowledges that our attitude towards bacteria is not a simple one.
sentences.translation: 这本书也承认，我们对细菌的态度并非简单单一。

structure.text: that our attitude towards bacteria is not a simple one

difficult_points: that 引导 acknowledges 的宾语从句。
one 代替前面的 attitude，避免重复。

sentences.sentence: We tend to see the dangers posed by bacteria, yet at the same time we are sold yoghurts and drinks that supposedly nurture 'friendly' bacteria.
sentences.translation: 我们往往只看到细菌带来的危险，但与此同时，商家又向我们推销据称能够滋养“有益”细菌的酸奶和饮料。

structure.text: We tend to see the dangers
yet at the same time we are sold yoghurts and drinks

difficult_points: posed by bacteria 是过去分词短语，修饰 the dangers。
we are sold ... 是双宾语 sell someone something 的被动形式，表示有人向我们推销。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 yoghurts and drinks；supposedly 表示这种效果只是宣称如此。

sentences.sentence: In reality, says Yong, bacteria should not be viewed as either friends or foes, villains or heroes.
sentences.translation: Yong 说，实际上，我们不应把细菌简单地视为朋友或敌人、恶棍或英雄。

structure.text: should not be viewed as either friends or foes, villains or heroes

difficult_points: says Yong 是倒装形式的插入语，主干从 bacteria 开始。
be viewed as 表示“被视为”；either ... or ... 在此连接对立类别。
friends or foes 与 villains or heroes 是两组并列的二元判断。

sentences.sentence: Instead we should realise we have a symbiotic relationship, that can be mutually beneficial or mutually destructive.
sentences.translation: 相反，我们应该认识到，我们与细菌之间存在一种共生关系，而这种关系可能互利，也可能彼此有害。

structure.text: that can be mutually beneficial or mutually destructive

difficult_points: realise 后省略 that，we have ... 是其宾语从句。
逗号后的 that 从句修饰 a symbiotic relationship，说明这种关系可能产生的两种结果。
mutually 同时修饰 beneficial 和 destructive，强调影响是双方相互的。

sentences.sentence: What then do these millions of organisms do?
sentences.translation: 那么，这数以百万计的生物究竟有什么作用呢？

structure.text: do these millions of organisms do

difficult_points: 第一个 What 是疑问代词，作第二个 do 的宾语。
第一个 do 是构成疑问句的助动词，第二个 do 是实义动词“做、发挥作用”。

sentences.sentence: The answer is pretty much everything.
sentences.translation: 答案是：几乎什么都做。
difficult_points: everything 呼应上一句的 do，意思是微生物几乎参与一切活动。
pretty much 是程度副词短语，表示“几乎、差不多”。

sentences.sentence: New research is now unravelling the ways in which bacteria aid digestion, regulate our immune systems, eliminate toxins, produce vitamins, affect our behaviour and even combat obesity.
sentences.translation: 新的研究正在逐步揭示细菌发挥作用的各种方式：它们帮助消化、调节免疫系统、清除毒素、产生维生素、影响我们的行为，甚至对抗肥胖。

structure.text: in which bacteria aid digestion, regulate our immune systems, eliminate toxins, produce vitamins, affect our behaviour and even combat obesity

difficult_points: unravelling 在这里指逐步弄清或揭示复杂机制。
in which 引导定语从句修饰 the ways，可理解为“细菌以这些方式……”。
aid、regulate、eliminate、produce、affect 和 combat 是以 bacteria 为主语的并列谓语。

sentences.sentence: 'They actually help us become who we are,' says Yong.
sentences.translation: “它们实际上帮助我们成为现在的自己，”Yong 说道。
difficult_points: help us become 后接省略 to 的不定式结构。
who we are 是名词性从句，作 become 的表语，表示“我们如今的样子”。
says Yong 是引语后的倒装报道语。

expressions.meaning_zh: 我们之所以成为现在的自己

sentences.sentence: But we are facing a growing problem.
sentences.translation: 但是，我们正面临一个日益严重的问题。
difficult_points: But 将语义从微生物的益处转向人类造成的问题。
growing 修饰 problem，表示问题的程度正在加剧。

expressions.meaning_zh: 面临一个日益严重的问题

sentences.sentence: Our obsession with hygiene, our overuse of antibiotics and our unhealthy, low-fibre diets are disrupting the bacterial balance and may be responsible for soaring rates of allergies and immune problems, such as inflammatory bowel disease (IBD).
sentences.translation: 我们对卫生的过度执着、对抗生素的滥用以及不健康的低纤维饮食，正在破坏细菌平衡，并可能是过敏和免疫问题发生率飙升的原因，例如炎症性肠病（IBD）。

structure.text: Our obsession with hygiene, our overuse of antibiotics and our unhealthy, low-fibre diets
and may be responsible for soaring rates of allergies and immune problems

difficult_points: 主语由三个并列名词短语构成，因此谓语使用复数 are。
are disrupting 和 may be responsible for 是由 and 连接的并列谓语。
soaring rates 指发生率急剧上升；such as 后举出 immune problems 的例子。

sentences.sentence: The most recent research actually turns accepted norms upside down.
sentences.translation: 最新的研究实际上彻底颠覆了人们公认的观念。
difficult_points: accepted norms 指已经被普遍接受的观念或常规。
turn A upside down 是“动词 + 宾语 + 宾补”结构，这里比喻彻底颠覆。

sentences.sentence: For example, there are studies indicating that the excessive use of household detergents and antibacterial products actually destroys the microbes that normally keep the more dangerous germs at bay.
sentences.translation: 例如，有研究表明，过度使用家用清洁剂和抗菌产品，实际上会消灭那些通常能够遏制更危险病菌的微生物。

structure.text: that the excessive use of household detergents and antibacterial products actually destroys the microbes
that normally keep the more dangerous germs at bay

difficult_points: indicating ... 是现在分词短语修饰 studies，说明研究表明的内容。
第一个 that 引导 indicating 的宾语从句，第二个 that 引导定语从句修饰 microbes。
主语中心词是单数 use，因此谓语用 destroys，而不是 destroy。

sentences.sentence: Other studies show that keeping a dog as a pet gives children early exposure to a diverse range of bacteria, which may help protect them against allergies later.
sentences.translation: 另一些研究显示，把狗作为宠物饲养能让孩子较早接触多种细菌，而这可能有助于保护他们日后免受过敏困扰。

structure.text: that keeping a dog as a pet gives children early exposure to a diverse range of bacteria
keeping a dog as a pet
which may help protect them against allergies later

difficult_points: that 引导 show 的宾语从句，其中动名词短语 keeping ... 作主语。
give children early exposure to 是双宾语式结构，表示让孩子较早接触。
which 指代前面让孩子接触多种细菌这件事；them 指 children。

sentences.sentence: The readers of Yong's book must be prepared for a decidedly unglamorous world.
sentences.translation: Yong 这本书的读者必须做好准备，面对一个显然毫无光鲜可言的世界。
difficult_points: be prepared for 表示对将要面对的情况做好心理准备。
decidedly 在此加强语气，表示这个世界“显然、确实”并不光鲜。

sentences.sentence: Among the less appealing case studies is one about a fungus that is wiping out entire populations of frogs and that can be halted by a rare microbial bacterium.
sentences.translation: 那些不太令人愉快的案例中，有一个讲的是某种真菌：它正在使整个青蛙种群灭绝，但一种罕见的微生物细菌能够阻止它。

structure.text: Among the less appealing case studies
that is wiping out entire populations of frogs
and that can be halted by a rare microbial bacterium

difficult_points: 地点范围状语 Among ... 置于句首引起完全倒装，主语是 one。
两个由 and 连接的 that 定语从句都修饰 fungus。
第二个定语从句使用被动语态，表示这种真菌可被一种细菌遏制。

sentences.sentence: Another is about squid that carry luminescent bacteria that protect them against predators.
sentences.translation: 另一个案例讲的是鱿鱼，它们携带的发光细菌能够保护它们免受捕食者攻击。
difficult_points: Another 省略了 case study，承接上一句。
第一个 that 从句修饰 squid，第二个 that 从句修饰 luminescent bacteria。
them 指代 squid，而非 bacteria。

sentences.sentence: However, if you can overcome your distaste for some of the investigations, the reasons for Yong's enthusiasm become clear.
sentences.translation: 不过，如果你能克服对其中一些研究的反感，就会明白 Yong 为何如此热衷于这个领域。

structure.text: if you can overcome your distaste for some of the investigations

difficult_points: if 引导条件状语从句，说明理解作者热情的前提。
distaste for 表示对某事的强烈不喜欢或反感。
the reasons ... become clear 的自然中文可转化为“明白为何……”。

sentences.sentence: The microbial world is a place of wonder.
sentences.translation: 微生物世界是一个充满奇迹的地方。
difficult_points: a place of wonder 是比喻性表语，强调微生物世界令人惊奇。
wonder 在这里是不可数概念，表示惊奇和奇妙，而非具体“疑问”。

sentences.sentence: Already, in an attempt to stop mosquitoes spreading dengue fever – a disease that infects 400 million people a year – mosquitoes are being loaded with a bacterium to block the disease.
sentences.translation: 目前，为了阻止蚊子传播登革热——这种疾病每年感染4亿人——人们正在把一种细菌植入蚊子体内，以阻断疾病传播。

structure.text: in an attempt to stop mosquitoes spreading dengue fever
that infects 400 million people a year
mosquitoes are being loaded with a bacterium

difficult_points: in an attempt to ... 是目的状语，其中 stop mosquitoes spreading 是“阻止某人或某物做某事”。
破折号中的 a disease 是 dengue fever 的同位语，that 从句进一步说明该疾病。
are being loaded 是现在进行时的被动语态，表示这一做法已经在实施。

sentences.sentence: In the future, our ability to manipulate microbes means we could construct buildings with useful microbes built into their walls to fight off infections.
sentences.translation: 未来，我们操控微生物的能力意味着，我们可以建造把有益微生物嵌入墙体的建筑，用它们来抵御感染。

structure.text: we could construct buildings with useful microbes built into their walls

difficult_points: to manipulate microbes 是不定式后置定语，说明 ability 的具体内容。
means 后省略 that，后面的完整句子作宾语从句。
with useful microbes built into their walls 是 with 复合结构，built 表示微生物被嵌入墙中。

sentences.sentence: Just imagine a neonatal hospital ward coated in a specially mixed cocktail of microbes so that babies get the best start in life.
sentences.translation: 试想一下：一间新生儿病房覆盖着专门调配的微生物组合，让婴儿在人生之初就获得最佳开端。

structure.text: coated in a specially mixed cocktail of microbes
so that babies get the best start in life

difficult_points: imagine 是祈使句谓语，主语 you 被省略。
coated ... 是过去分词短语作后置定语，修饰 hospital ward。
so that 引导目的状语从句；cocktail 在此比喻经过调配的微生物组合。

expressions.text: get the best start in life
